channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Families of Drug War Victims Hope for Justice as Duterte Faces ICC Trial
item.description: The families of children killed in the Philippines' drug war are hopeful for justice as former President Duterte faces trial at the International Criminal Court (ICC). Grace, whose 16-year-old daughte...

item.title: DSS Arraigns El-Rufai Over Alleged Phone Tapping of NSA Ribadu
item.description: The Department of State Services (DSS) has brought charges against Nasir El-Rufai, former governor of Kaduna State, before a Federal High Court in Abuja. The charges are related to his alleged involve...

item.title: Debt-ridden property dealer in UP poisons pet and ends life by suicide
item.description: A property dealer from Uttar Pradesh, facing significant financial pressure due to mounting debts, tragically ended his life by suicide after poisoning his pet dog. The man, who had been struggling wi...

item.title: Criminals are commercializing child abuse online and rely on human reporting to be caught
item.description: Online predators who distribute child sexual abuse material are increasingly operating like commercial enterprises, running fully commercial sites across the open web that sell access to vast quantiti...

item.title: U.S. House Oversight Committee Divided on Potential Ghislaine Maxwell Pardon
item.description: Members of the U.S. House Oversight Committee have expressed differing views on the possibility of a pardon for Ghislaine Maxwell, the convicted accomplice of financier Jeffrey Epstein. The committee,...

item.title: Tanzania ranks fourth in global malaria deaths according to World Malaria Report 2025
item.description: According to the World Malaria Report 2025, Tanzania is among the four countries with the highest number of malaria deaths worldwide in 2024. Out of about 600,000 malaria deaths globally, Nigeria acco...

item.title: Fernando Mendoza’s Rise from Overlooked Recruit to Projected No. 1 NFL Draft Pick
item.description: Fernando Mendoza has emerged as one of the most unexpected stars in recent football history, rising from an overlooked high school recruit to a projected No. 1 overall pick in the NFL Draft. Born to C...

item.title: Prince Louis turns eight as the Royal Family releases new birthday portrait from Cornwall holiday
item.description: The Prince and Princess of Wales have marked their son Prince Louis’s eighth birthday by releasing a new official photograph. The image, taken by trusted royal photographer Matt Porteous during a rece...

item.title: Political Leaders Debate the Potential Risks of Surveillance-Based Pricing in Canada
item.description: Surveillance pricing, sometimes called algorithmic or predatory pricing, has become a political talking point in Canada. Ontario NDP leader Marit Stiles highlighted the practice in a recent video, exp...

item.title: INEC Chairman Amupitan Speaks on Faith, Leadership Challenges, and 2027 Election Preparations
item.description: INEC Chairman, Prof. Joash Amupitan, talk say na strong spiritual conviction make am accept the responsibility to lead Nigeria electoral body. According to am, before he agree take the job, he receive...

item.title: Tanzania Expands Budget to Upgrade Roads and Boost Infrastructure
item.description: Tanzania government is working hard to strengthen the capacity of the Rural and Urban Roads Agency (TARURA) to accelerate construction of asphalt roads across the country. This move is aimed at improv...

item.title: Concerns over Canada's digital sovereignty and innovation in upcoming CUSMA renegotiations
item.description: In an opinion piece, John Ivison highlights growing concerns about Canada's position in the upcoming renegotiation of the Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement (CUSMA). Canada's chief trade negotiator...

item.title: Global oil supply disrupted as Iran conflict and Strait of Hormuz closure trigger energy crisis warnings
item.description: The head of the International Energy Agency (IEA), Fatih Birol, has warned that the ongoing conflict involving Iran has removed around 13 million barrels of oil per day from global supply, creating wh...

item.title: Autopsy reveals 14‑year‑old Celeste Rivas Hernandez died from multiple penetrating injuries in D4vd homicide case
item.description: A newly released autopsy report shows that 14‑year‑old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the alleged murder victim of singer D4vd (real name David Anthony Burke), died from multiple penetrating injuries caused...

item.title: ABC reports rise in food relief demand, Canavan makes waves in politics, and Farrer seat battle intensifies
item.description: In a recent report by the ABC, a startling claim was made that over one-third of Australians are seeking food relief for the first time. This figure, derived from Oz Harvest’s annual Frontline Report,...

item.title: Head-on collision between two local trains in northern Denmark leaves multiple injured
item.description: A serious rail accident occurred in northern Denmark when two local trains collided head-on between the towns of Hillerød and Kagerup, approximately 40 kilometres north of Copenhagen. Authorities conf...

item.title: BBC investigation uncovers open drug sales in West Midlands mini-marts
item.description: A BBC investigation has revealed that illegal drugs such as cannabis and cocaine are being openly sold in some High Street mini-marts in towns across the West Midlands. Undercover researchers filmed i...

item.title: Reports Reveal Widespread Abuse and Lack of Care During Childbirth in DR Congo
item.description: For Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), many women don begin speak out about serious abuse and suffering wey dem face during childbirth for hospitals. Dis matter come to public eye after one viral vid...

item.title: Farmers adjust hay harvest timing to protect nesting threatened bird species
item.description: Some farmers are choosing to delay their annual hay harvest in an effort to protect threatened grassland bird species during their nesting season. In many parts of Canada, birds such as bobolinks and ...

item.title: Comelec mandates birth certificate submission for candidates in BSKE and upcoming elections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) announced that all candidates filing their certificates of candidacy (COC) for the 2026 Barangay and Sangguniang Kabataan Elections (BSKE), as well as future elec...
